generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use visible AADL features and declared equation helpers within the AGREE annex to avoid unresolved reference errors.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are used correctly for constants and equations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use AGREE logical operators like `and`, `or`, `not`, and `=>` for contract construction.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence: `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`.
anti_patterns: Avoid using implementation-level subcomponent paths within component type annexes.
Do not introduce new architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing expressions and types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
Compact requirement semantics and model traceability
AGREE assumption/guarantee placement in component types vs. implementations
